info.title: Docker Infra API
info.description: Initial API contract for Docker Infra. Secret values are never included in examples.

tags.name: system
tags.description: System health, OpenAPI, and Swagger UI

get.summary: Ensure local master using default options

post.summary: Ensure local Docker Infra host is the Swarm manager and overlay network exists
Register or update a managed node
post.description: Create a new managed node when `node_id` is omitted. Update an existing non-master node when `node_id` is supplied. Password is required for first-time registration and optional when reusing a stored SSH key during update.

delete.description: Unregister a non-master node after remote cleanup. `confirmation_name` must exactly match the current node name.

post.summary: Check slave SSH and Docker daemon status
Run Swarm join for a slave node
Prepare automatic node status collection token

get.summary: List node metrics ordered by latest report time
Return containers from the latest node metric

post.summary: Ingest node metric from a reporter token
Validate and normalize a Docker Compose document

example.content:
services:
  web:
    image: nginx:1.27
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "true"]

sessionCookie.name: docker_infra_session
sessionCookie.description: Password-only login session cookie. The login API itself does not require a user ID.

masked_value.description: Masked placeholder only; never return the original secret.

private_host.description: Address other Docker Infra nodes should use to reach the local master.

public_ip.description: Public IP used for service DNS records when configured.

private_host.description: Address other Docker Infra nodes should use to reach this node.

public_ip.description: Public IP used for service DNS records when configured.

private_address.description: Private/internal address other nodes should use to join the Swarm manager.

confirmation_name.description: Current node name typed exactly by the user.

private_address.description: Private/internal address other nodes should use to reach the local master.

x-ai-agent.description: AI Agent executable operation catalog. Read operations may run directly; write operations require explicit user intent; destructive operations require exact confirmation before execution.
